core_principle: A constraint or prohibition must outrank any presence-based type. Category + Level are only supporting context.

groups.rationale: Grounding text is high-risk because presence evidence can look strong while still being incomplete or misleading.
Minimum, maximum, length, and separation language cannot be satisfied by generic element counts or level assignment.

groups.group_name: Manufacturer, Owner Standard, and Product Spec Restrictions
groups.rationale: Brand lists, approved-equal language, base-bid / alt-bid restrictions, and product-data requirements are specification problems, not presence problems.

groups.group_name: Controls, DDC, EMCS, BAS, and BMS
groups.rationale: Controls language changes the evidence requirement. If the text asks for DDC/EMCS or a sequence, equipment presence does not close it.

groups.group_name: Installation Method, Field Execution, Demolition, Protection
groups.rationale: These requirements are about how work is executed in the field, not what the model contains.
Drawings, approvals, code matrices, commissioning, O&M, and spare-parts deliverables are external evidence sets and must not be converted into model presence.
Tags, labels, marks, and nameplates are physical or document-based requirements, not generic category evidence.
These types are the only ones that should be allowed to close from model evidence, and even then only when the required parameters are present.
Fallback only. This should be decomposed as the taxonomy matures.
